seo.title: Building Code Generation with LangChain + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with LangChain + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: LangChain simplifies building LLM-powered code generation agents that write, test, and refine code iteratively. However, without guardrails, these agents risk generating unsafe code, leaking credentials, or entering infinite loops during execution and refinement. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure code safety, syntax validity, and secure execution in production.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with LangChain
architectureOverview.description:
A typical LangChain code generation agent uses an LLM (like GPT-4) prompted to generate code based on user requirements. The agent integrates tools for sandboxed code execution (using Docker or E2B), test validation via unittest/pytest, and iterative refinement through agent loops. LangChain's AgentExecutor manages the ReAct pattern: Reason, Act (generate code), Observe (execute and test), and repeat until validation succeeds.

The workflow starts with a user request parsed into structured requirements. The LLM generates code, which is executed in a sandbox. Results and test outcomes feed back into the agent for refinement. Retrieval chains pull relevant code examples or libraries from a vector store to reduce hallucinations. Context management prevents token overflow during long refinement cycles.

Key components include: a custom CodeGenerationTool for sandbox execution, a TestValidatorTool for running unit tests, and structured output parsers ensuring consistent code formatting across iterations.

risks.name: Malicious Code Injection
risks.description: Agent generates code with system calls, file deletions, or network requests that could harm the host environment
risks.mitigation: Vex sandbox verification + static analysis before execution

risks.name: Credential Leakage
risks.description: Generated code accidentally embeds API keys, passwords, or secrets from training data or context
risks.mitigation: Vex regex scanning + secret detection across all chain outputs

risks.name: Infinite Refinement Loops
risks.description: Agent enters tool call loops when tests continuously fail without convergence
risks.mitigation: Vex loop detection + max iterations enforcement

risks.name: Unsafe Dependencies
risks.description: Code installs malicious packages or uses vulnerable library versions

implementation.title: Basic Code Generation Chain
implementation.description: Simple LangChain chain that generates Python code from natural language requirements
implementation.code:
from langchain.prompts import PromptTemplate
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain.schema.output_parser import StrOutputParser

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4", temperature=0)

prompt = PromptTemplate(
    input_variables=["requirements"],
    template="""Write clean Python code that {requirements}.

Return ONLY the code, no explanations:
"""
)

code_chain = prompt | llm | StrOutputParser()

result = code_chain.invoke({"requirements": "sorts a list of integers using quicksort"})
print(result)

implementation.title: Agent with Sandbox Tool
implementation.description: LangChain agent with custom sandbox execution tool for safe code testing
implementation.code:
from langchain.agents import create_react_agent, AgentExecutor
from langchain.tools import tool
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
import subprocess
import tempfile

@tool
def execute_code(code: str) -> str:
    """Execute Python code in sandbox and return results."""
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(mode='w', suffix='.py', delete=False) as f:
        f.write(code)
        temp_file = f.name
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(['python', temp_file], 
                              capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10)
        return f"STDOUT: {result.stdout}\nSTDERR: {result.stderr}\nEXIT: {result.returncode}"
    finally:
        import os
        os.unlink(temp_file)

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4")
agent = create_react_agent(llm, [execute_code], prompt)
agent_executor = AgentExecutor(agent=agent, tools=[execute_code], verbose=True)

result = agent_executor.invoke({"input": "Write and test code that reverses a string"})

implementation.description: LangChain.js agent for code generation with structured output parsing

implementation.title: Test Validation Tool
implementation.description: Custom tool that validates generated code with unit tests
implementation.code:
from langchain.tools import tool
import pytest
import sys
from io import StringIO

@tool
def validate_with_tests(code: str, test_code: str) -> str:
    """Run unit tests against generated code. Returns PASS/FAIL status."""
    combined = code + "\n\n" + test_code
    old_stdout = sys.stdout
    sys.stdout = mystdout = StringIO()
    
    try:
        exec(combined)
        pytest.main(["-v", "--tb=no"])
        output = mystdout.getvalue()
        return "PASS" if "passed" in output.lower() else "FAIL: " + output[:500]
    except Exception as e:
        return f"ERROR: {str(e)}"
    finally:
        sys.stdout = old_stdout
